notes: Assistant-drafted seed labels for pi.dev website retrieval experiments. Use for cross-site calibration only until reviewed.

cases.question: Where does Pi document quickstart installation, subscription login or API key setup, project instructions, and running shell commands?

judgments.reason: Quickstart covers install options, subscription/API key auth, project instructions, shell commands, model switching, and non-interactive mode.
Usage is related to running Pi after setup.

cases.question: Which Pi page explains usage options such as modes, model options, session options, tool options, resource options, file arguments, and examples?

judgments.reason: Using Pi page covers editor features, system prompt files, project trust, modes, model/session/tool/resource options, file arguments, and examples.
Settings is adjacent configuration material.

cases.question: Where are Pi model providers documented, including OpenAI Codex, Claude Pro or Max, GitHub Copilot, Azure OpenAI, and Amazon Bedrock?

judgments.reason: Providers page documents built-in provider setup for OpenAI Codex, Claude, GitHub Copilot, Azure OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, and auth sources.
Custom models page is related model configuration material.

cases.question: Which Pi docs page lists settings for telemetry, network, retry, shell, sessions, markdown, terminal images, and model cycling?

judgments.reason: Settings page enumerates UI/display, telemetry/update checks, network, retry, shell, sessions, model cycling, markdown, and terminal/image settings.
Usage page covers runtime options that overlap with settings.

cases.question: Where does Pi document keybindings for TUI editor movement, deletion, input, kill ring, clipboard selection, sessions, models, and tree navigation?

judgments.reason: Keybindings page lists editor movement/deletion/input, kill ring, clipboard/selection, sessions, models, display, tree navigation, and examples.
TUI components are adjacent UI documentation.

cases.question: Which Pi page explains extensions, async factory functions, long-lived resources, startup events, project_trust, resource discovery, and session lifecycle events?

judgments.reason: Extensions page covers async factory functions, long-lived resources, lifecycle overview, startup/project/resource/session events, and shutdown.
SDK page is related integration material.

cases.question: Where are Pi skills documented, including SKILL.md format, name rules, description best practices, setup, usage, and compatibility with other harnesses?

judgments.reason: Skills page documents using skills, SKILL.md format, name rules, description best practices, setup, usage, and examples.
Packages can distribute skills and related extension artifacts.

cases.question: Which Pi documentation covers packages from npm, git, ssh, version refs, local paths, gallery metadata, and convention directories?

judgments.reason: Pi Packages page covers npm, git, ssh shorthand/protocols, version refs, local paths, gallery metadata, and convention directories.
Packages gallery is related but not the documentation page.

cases.question: Where does Pi explain custom providers, API types, auth headers, OAuth callbacks and credentials, stream patterns, tool calls, usage and cost, and registration?

judgments.reason: Custom Providers page covers API types, auth headers, OAuth callbacks/credentials, stream patterns, content blocks, tool calls, usage/cost, overflow, and registration.
Providers page is related provider configuration material.

cases.question: Which Pi SDK page documents createAgentSession, AgentSessionRuntime, prompting, message queueing, events, directories, tools, extensions, and skills?

judgments.reason: SDK page documents createAgentSession, AgentSession, runtime, prompting/message queueing, events, directories, tools, extensions, and skills.
Extensions page is adjacent SDK/integration documentation.
